content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 5,
1993
Honorable Richard G.
Polanco
Assistant Speaker pro Tempore
110 North Avenue
56
Los Angeles,
CA
90042
Re:
Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice.
Our File No.
A-93-144
Dear Assemblyman Polanco:
We have received your two letters dated April 9,
1993,
requesting written confirmation of the telephone advice I provided
to Mr.
Marco Firebaugh of your office on April 9,
1993,
regarding
the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.
(Government Code Sections 81000-91015.)
Your letters accurately summarize the advice that I provided
to Mr.
Firebaugh.
I advised Mr.
Firebaugh that your controlled
committee may make unlimited independent expenditures to produce a
video in support of
a candidate running in the Los Angeles mayoral
election.
I also advised Mr.
Firebaugh that the photograph
provided to your committee by an agent of the candidate, for use in
the video would not,
by itself, result in the video being deemed
a
contribution to the candidate.
This advice is based on the
conclusions and analyses contained in the enclosed FPPC advice
letters issued to Judith Sproul Davis,
No.
1-90-173,
and Robert
E.
Leidigh,
No.
1-92—547.
Mr.
Firebaugh received faxed copies of
these two letters on April 9,
1993.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please contact me at 916/322—5662.
Sincerely,
Jeff Marschner
Ger)eral Qe.se1
Enclosures
cc:
Legislative Office
State Capitol
By:
Ann Kvasager
tical Reform Consultant
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660

embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission May 5, 1993 Honorable Richard G. Polanco Assistant Speaker pro Tempore 110 North Avenue 56 Los Angeles, CA 90042 Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice. Our File No. A-93-144 Dear Assemblyman Polanco: We have received your two letters dated April 9, 1993, requesting written confirmation of the telephone advice I provided to Mr. Marco Firebaugh of your office on April 9, 1993, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.) Your letters accurately summarize the advice that I provided to Mr. Firebaugh. I advised Mr. Firebaugh that your controlled committee may make unlimited independent expenditures to produce a video in support of a candidate running in the Los Angeles mayoral election. I also advised Mr. Firebaugh that the photograph provided to your committee by an agent of the candidate, for use in the video would not, by itself, result in the video being deemed a contribution to the candidate. This advice is based on the conclusions and analyses contained in the enclosed FPPC advice letters issued to Judith Sproul Davis, No. 1-90-173, and Robert E. Leidigh, No. 1-92—547. Mr. Firebaugh received faxed copies of these two letters on April 9, 1993. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at 916/322—5662. Sincerely, Jeff Marschner Ger)eral Qe.se1 Enclosures cc: Legislative Office State Capitol By: Ann Kvasager tical Reform Consultant 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
